text:
It was a bright, sunny day. I sat under an old oak tree in the park, reading a dusty book. Suddenly, a small bird landed on my shoulder. I pet it. "Oh," it chirped. "You want to hear a secret?" I nodded. "You see," it began, fluffing up its feathers, "I've never sung."

I smirked. "Not like that."

"The secret?" It fluttered its wings. "I've sung to all the trees in this forest."

I laughed. "You?"

"It's easy." It tilted its head. "Just listen." It sang a melody like wind through willows. I listened attentively. It sang a love song, then a lullaby. Finally, it launched into a song of gratitude.

"You've sung?" I asked.

"It can do that," it said. "I'm an Emberfinch. We've got beautiful songs."

We continued to sing.

Hours went by.
